etymology.description: 寸 (hand indicating measurement) with 肉 (meat/flesh) added for distinction -> 肘
etymology.interpretation: The character 肘(肘) (zhǒu, "elbow") combines 寸(寸) (cùn, "inch"), originally depicting a hand with a stroke for the elbow, with 肉(肉) (ròu, "meat") to specifically indicate the fleshiness of the elbow area in the human body.

examples.pinyin: Tā zhuàng dào le wǒ de zhǒu.

examples.chinese: 在瑜伽课上(在瑜伽課上)，我们学习如何将肘(肘)贴地(貼地)。
examples.pinyin: Zài yújiākè shàng, wǒmen xuéxí rúhé jiāng zhǒu tiē dì.
examples.english: In yoga class, we learn how to place our elbows on the ground.

examples.chinese: 医生建议我(醫生建議我)，在锻炼时(在鍛鍊時)保护好肘关节(肘關節)。
examples.pinyin: Yīshēng jiànyì wǒ, zài duànliàn shí bǎohù hǎo zhǒu guānjié.
examples.english: The doctor advised me to protect my elbow joint during exercise.
